sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Event-specific mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Game consists of multiple sports events (weightlifting, slalom skiing, log rolling, cliff diving, caber toss, bull riding, barrel jumping, sumo wrestling) each with distinct control schemes

physics_values.note: Mechanics are event-specific; documented as 'bizarre little joystick patterns which have little to do with the events' rather than realistic physics simulation

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/qualitative data only. No detailed physics documentation available. Sources reference gameplay mechanics as abstract joystick patterns rather than simulation-based movement physics. NES port by Software Creations (1989) based on original Epyx design (1986). Game focuses on sports mini-games with event-specific controls rather than consistent character physics.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - World Games (NES)
